description: A low-intensity blast near a railway track in Patiala late Monday night has sparked panic. An unidentified, dismembered body was found nearby, though its connection to the explosion is under investigation. The blast caused minor damage to the freight train line. Forensic teams are examining the scene for evidence.

description: At an NRAI event in Delhi, Manu Bhaker was asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, sparking online criticism for shifting focus from her Olympic achievements. Meanwhile, Sooryavanshi continues to shine, becoming the youngest to 1,000 T20 runs and emerging as a promising future star for India.

The movie's performance reflects a decline after its initial surge.

description: Following tragic family deaths in Bhendi Bazaar, doctors reassured the public that watermelons are safe, emphasizing their high water content and hydrating properties. Experts stated that even stale watermelons are unlikely to be fatal, drawing a distinction from rare incidents involving bitter cucumbers. There is no need for widespread panic regarding this popular summer fruit.

description: Serena Williams shared a real parenting moment where she had to discipline her daughter, even though it made them both cry. description: The Dallas Cowboys face a crucial decision regarding wide receiver George Pickens. CBS Sports suggests a trade. ClutchPoints suggest Pickens could be acquired in exchange for draft capital and wide receiver Keon Coleman. However, recent reports indicate the Buffalo Bills are committed to WR Keon Coleman. This alters the proposed trade scenario and leaving Pickens’ future with Dallas uncertain. But he wants a trade. So, we are back to square one.

description: India and New Zealand have signed a 'once-in-a-generation' trade pact, granting Indian exporters duty-free access to New Zealand. The agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $5 billion in five years and includes a $20 billion investment commitment from New Zealand. Both nations are optimistic about the pact's potential to boost economic growth and cooperation across various sectors.

Disney, Warner Brothers Discovery & others to EU: Upcoming act should target Big Tech
description: Major European media companies are urging EU officials to reconsider a new law. They fear the Digital Fairness Act, aimed at online consumer protection, could unintentionally damage their businesses. The companies argue that existing regulations already cover their operations. They want the focus to be on large tech firms where responsibility gaps exist, not on established media sectors.

title: South Bengal’s silent voter and the storm it may hide
Fact check: Clavicular pregnancy rumour after 10-day romance debunked
description: Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Peters, went viral after claims he is expecting a child with a girlfriend of 10 days. The rumor came from a viral post and a misunderstood livestream. There is no proof of pregnancy. The story is false, while real concerns remain about his health, recent hospital stay, and ongoing controversies.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
description: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.
